Good, how are you?
Good.
I guess we'll started.
Let me just share my screen.
Okay, so just a quick recap.
So we delivered six articles for review last week.

Raveena published those, I think, yesterday.
So those were done as well.
We addressed all edits.
And just as an update, we addressed your edits, Matt and Cody, in the e-book as well.
So the only thing is, like, some of the edits around the images, the way it works is we generated those images, but we can't edit the images themselves.
So we left some suggestions, maybe just asking if your designer could move things around a bit.
Sure.
Awesome.
you.

Working on five articles this week, and as part of just Matt's suggestion, last week, we're exploring whether we can find something in the data analyst skills space just to see if we can piggyback on any momentum over there.
Jumping to the performance update this week, let me show you Luca.
So generally, we had another week where, you know, clicks and impressions increased.
So things are going quite well.
We're obviously building momentum.
A big driver of this is obviously still the data engineering skills article.
But we are seeing a lot of other articles also pick up the pace in terms of the, you know, some of the older articles like the ETL, ELT.
We had refresh those.
So those blogs are doing quite well.
Some of our other blogs like data pipelines as well are picking up the pace and gathering some momentum.
So that's nice.
There is.
is.
A bit of a gap between GA4 and GSE, as usual.
I think it's just the way they attribute clicks that's causing some of this confusion.
But generally, despite that, even in GA4, we're seeing like we've had the second highest number of views and sessions.
So generally, things are looking quite good.
We are obviously headed in the right direction.
Something else to note is that the number of returned direct visitors.
So again, as a reminder, these are people who consumed our content and then basically returned to Prophecy's website by directly typing in the URL, their browsers.
This saw a nice little leap over here.
So it's pretty encouraging generally around this time is when we'd expect SEO results to start compounding and, you know, see some of these metrics for movement.
So it seems to be happening kind of on schedule, which is a nice thing.
Some general observations, sorry, is that for some reasons our articles are taking a little while to gain some momentum.
I'm not quite sure why that is.

It might be a function of the general level of competitiveness in the keywords we're aiming for, the brand authorities in some of these searches, because for a lot of these topics, even though it's not a one-to-one sort of match to what proxy does, we're companies like IBM and, you know, obviously Informatica shows up as well.
So we're thinking, like, the slower momentum might be because of these heavyweight brands being present.

So even if, you know, Ahrefs or Semra shows that, okay, this keyword is easy to rank for, just from a human perspective, like, when I see the search results, if I see IBM there, I'm just more likely to click on it.
So we are going up against, you know, some pretty hefty competition, and I think that's why it's the momentum.
It takes a while for these articles to really just flex them and then build up some momentum.
In terms of LLM referrals, it fell this week, but generally, there's nothing to worry about in that sense.
We still are getting a good number of referrals from there and generally seem to be doing the right things.
So any questions I can answer about all of this?
Any clarifications?

Yeah, so I was, there were some other, and Natasha and Cody, didn't actually share this with you, but Justin, who we used to work with at Arcule, posted a little bit about the GA4 and the search console discrepancy.
Is that accurate?
Or is that, I mean, I know you're saying it exists.
Is there a way to kind of, do we know what, what does it mean?
Like, because if each tool is different, but they're consistent, that's one thing.
See, Lars
We are consistent.
So there are some broad-based differences and there are some minutia in there as well.
So broad-based, GA4 is measuring everything.
It's not just looking at Google search.
GSC looks only at Google search and basically how we're showing up in the search engine results pages.
GA4 is looking at what's happening within the website.
Like once somebody lands on prophecy's website, what's going on?
What are they doing exactly?
The discrepancy happens because of the sampling issues, the data sampling issues that just have always existed between GSC and GA4.
What happened is GSC is kind of, we can think of it as a legacy tool.
It was built for search that existed back in like 2011, 2012, when there was no real analytics.
It was just like, okay, how many clicks am I getting?
How often am I showing up?
That's it.
And it just kind of remained.
GA4, meanwhile, has gone through multiple iterations, and they've kind of improved that product far more than they had Search Console.
So as a result, the way they measure data, the way they sort of even think about measuring sessions and measuring user interaction and behavior is completely different.
So right down to the point where even the timeframes are different.
Like GSC data, you can see from this image over here, it's saying week 20, but in GA4, it'll say like week 21 or week 22.
So it's just like an inherent discrepancy, and that remains consistent.
The inconsistencies of late have been happening because GSC or Search Console's legacy features have begun showing up, especially now that AI overviews are, you know, increasing.
What's happening is that GSC just does not have enough data for whatever reason.
It's not capturing clicks and impressions as accurately as it used to.
That's the top.
process anyway.

And we're seeing this across all of our accounts where impressions, actually for us, we're not seeing that much of a discrepancy, but for some of the other accounts, we're seeing like impressions absolutely shoot up, like just go through the roof and then suddenly fall down the next week.
A lot of volatility.
It's not that, you know, the articles are performing bad or performing poorly.
It's just that there's the way GSC is measuring that data gathering is a problem.
So is this something we're kind of living through?
The way we're mitigating it is looking at both.
So as long as they kind of make sense, we're happy with it.
hope that.

Well, we can't trust just one of them, but if we look at both of them, and they're largely saying the same thing, we can trust it.
So, for example, we look at the charts now in Looker, what's happening is that GA4 and GSE are saying the same thing, just that the extent to which this bar has increased is different, right?

So, if I pull it up real quick, GSE is saying like, this is our highest traffic week, sorry, GA4 is saying this is our highest traffic week, whereas GSE is saying that our latest week is the highest traffic week.
Ultimately, these two, we got a bunch of visitors, it's just that how are we distributing them?
So, they're largely saying the same thing.
Yep.
Yep, something to think about.
And thanks for that.
Yep.
So, yeah, it's one of the reasons why we're trying to use different data points just to map, like, which way we're going.
It's always been a challenge with SEO and content.
So, yeah, we're just doing it.
was easy, we wouldn't be here.
Exactly.
So, yeah, any other questions about all of this?
All right.
The talking point, really, for this week was the full comparison articles that I raised last week.

So I was wondering, are we comfortable creating this kind of thing, product comparisons, or do we still want to stay away from this?
I can address that.
We're going to stay away from it at the moment.
It is just far too risky to, from a brand perspective, to be viewed as a site that is, like, look at these 20 ETL tools and data integration tools, and, like, this is great at this.
Like, it just, there's too many variables, too many things to consider.
In general, we don't want to talk about any, quote-unquote, competitor that is smaller than us, ever, because we don't want to bring attention to somebody that's smaller than us.
But we won't, you know, we wouldn't mind talking about bigger fish.
And so it's, it'll, you know, we'll eventually write about these competitors, but...
Doing it in some programmatic way and putting out a list of 20 tools is not the direction we want to take from a brand perspective.
So it's basically a no on that, even if it would generate a bunch of traffic from LLMs.
Okay.
Thank you for that.
So, yeah, that was the main clarification we needed.

You know, especially the e-book.
We were quite happy to hear your feedback last week.
If you have anything additional there, I'm more than happy to hear it.
Change things up on our end.
Do you want to take a second?
I'm sorry.
Go ahead, Natasha.
It's all good.
So, yeah, the e-book, you know, maybe we can chat about that.
It, I think we all took a look at it now.
At least three of us did.
And, you know, it looked pretty good.
We were having some internal debates, and maybe this is not.
some debates, debates, debates, and we're internal and we're internal
I don't think we can answer here, but just putting it out there, like, what's the difference between a white paper and an e-book?
Like, does it really matter?
Do we need to have an author applied from a prophecy perspective, or can we just leave it as a prophecy-branded asset with no author?
And, you know, any thoughts on that would be appreciated.
Yeah, so those are just some of the open questions.

I don't know.

No, I would imagine the way that I typically would think about it is white paper tends to be, like, slightly more targeted and niche.

But that's kind of, like, rather broad.

But I think the line is kind of blurry.
Yeah, makes sense.
Okay.
Yeah, I'm viewing e-book as potentially, I don't know, I guess they're both top of the funnel, but I think you're right.

You might have some prior knowledge about the domain and come in with a certain set of expectations.
And again, I don't know if this stuff matters, like how we brand this thing, like an e-book just seems more accessible.
A white paper is like, okay, I'm going to sit down and really like learn something.
And so it matters from the perspective of it carries some connotation and weight in the people that are.
Downloading this stuff.
And so when you, I guess the question is, when you put this together, like, how did you provide the direction to around what you did, around, let's say, 24 pages long?
Like, that is a, that is a very long e-book.
Like, are we borderline white paper territory here?
Or does the length not really matter to you?
Just, I guess, I guess the question is, give me some insight into, into, into the direction.
Around how, how you generated this thing?
Um, from our thing, what we did is we, one of the reasons it's 24 pages long is because we just have, like, the five dysfunctions.
There's, like, five separate, um, items to address over there.
And I think we, when generating those, we deliberately tried to keep it really concise.
It's just that there's five of them, and that kind of expanded the length massively.
I don't think there's any significant technical material.

We kept it quite approachable and just kept it very short, like, this is a sort of problem, this is the case study that we're introducing, this is the solution, here's the proof, and that's the same format.
So I think that explains the length.
The individual sections themselves are pretty straightforward.

So in terms of the other approaches we took to sort of, how do you say, regenerating this was just, as I said, just keep it really succinct and on point, because we didn't want to write a novel over here.
I don't know, does that help at all?
Yeah, no, that's good.
So, yeah, I think the material is accessible.
It was more just a question around length.
Like, most of the e-books I see are, I don't five pages, ten pages, and, like, here, it's really, that's part of what makes it accessible.
And so we might be debating semantics at this point, but it's just...

I think the design might be one way of potentially reducing the length, maybe exploring multiple columns on the page, perhaps, maybe using more visuals, I don't know, we could certainly help you come up with those ideas, but instead of just going with the usual document format, maybe rethinking it as like a bunch of slides where you have multiple columns and like one, major column or as a graphic, that might certainly reduce the length.
I can send over an example of one particular e-book that I saw that does this.
It actually reads kind of like a presentation, but it still tells a story.
I don't know if that would be appropriate, that might be too big of , but certainly it would give some ideas into alternative formats and how you can use that to reduce the length of it.
Okay, yeah, send me over a month.
Suggesting we do any major surgery to it at this point.
think it's, again, semantics, but just useful, good thinking.
Well, no, I mean, I think the packaging, yeah, the packaging matters.
And I do, I mean, just looking, because I've been, you know, into those sections, but I kind of get where you're going, Vivek, on the idea where, you know, there are five things.

And so each one, if we can package it so mentally people are looking at it as five things, then it's not going to be a 30-page document.
It's going to be five seconds.
So I think, Mattesh, think we should, yeah, we'd like to have that as a model.

Okay.
But process-wise, we're in good shape.
All right.
Glad to hear that.
That was it for our sync.
I will follow up on Slack regarding the sample white paper.
got to dig it up and I'll send it over.

I didn't do it more.

you know, having, knowing kind of like, this is my idea, is that this is, you know, we'll flesh this out, but we're going to get information on impression and clicks from Search Console.

that was kind of two things that I'm thinking about.

From the whole search thing.

I think to your point about, is there a way to connect the clicks and impressions to GA4, drill down from that article, and then look at the keywords and all of this?
There is a way of doing it, which is super complicated.

I did something like that as a pass.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Prophecy and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, performance metrics, and upcoming initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content performance continues to improve, with clicks and impressions increasing across articles](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
  - [The team will avoid creating comparison articles listing multiple tools due to brand positioning concerns](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [E-book feedback was positive; discussions ongoing about formatting and branding (e-book vs. whitepaper)](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [Matt is developing a comprehensive dashboard to track content performance across various channels](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1127.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Update

  - [Six articles delivered for review last week; some published yesterday](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=20.0)
  - [Clicks and impressions increased; data engineering skills article remains a top performer](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [Older refreshed articles (ETL, ELT) and data pipelines content gaining momentum](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=98.0)
  - [GA4 shows second-highest number of views and sessions](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [Returned direct visitors saw a notable increase](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=162.0)
  - [Articles taking longer to gain traction, possibly due to competition from established brands](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)

### Analytics Discrepancies

  - [Differences exist between Google Search Console (GSC) and Google Analytics 4 (GA4) data](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=307.0)
  - [GSC focuses on search performance, while GA4 tracks on-site behavior](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [Sampling issues and legacy features in GSC contribute to discrepancies](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
  - [Team mitigates by analyzing both sources for consistent trends](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)

### Content Strategy Discussion

  - [Decision to avoid creating comparison articles listing multiple tools](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [Concerns about brand positioning and inadvertently promoting smaller competitors](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [Focus on maintaining Prophecy's current content approach](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)

### E-book Feedback and Formatting

  - [Overall positive reception to the e-book content](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
  - [Discussions about differentiating between e-book and whitepaper formats](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Current e-book length (24 pages) due to covering five distinct topics](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)
  - [Exploring design options to make content more visually appealing and concise](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=963.0)
  - [Team to review sample formats for inspiration](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)

### Performance Tracking Initiative

  - [Matt developing a comprehensive dashboard to track content performance](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1127.0)
  - [Aims to integrate data from various sources (GSC, GA4, ads, Blackbird, LinkedIn)](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1162.0)
  - [Focus on establishing consistent metrics and understanding user journey](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)
  - [Acknowledging challenges in directly linking data between different platforms](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1378.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Vivek to send sample e-book/whitepaper format for team review](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1397.0)
  - [Team to continue refining e-book design and formatting](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
  - [Matt to further develop and share the comprehensive performance tracking dashboard](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
  - [Continue monitoring content performance and adjusting strategy as needed](https://fathom.video/share/axfmrftzyBVdv3TgJ5zxcPazWNt_vWRM?tab=summary&timestamp=1470.0)

action_items.description: Send sample e-book/white paper with alternative format (presentation-like, multiple columns) to Mitesh via Slack
Review e-book content one final time, consolidate feedback for design team
Flesh out data reporting framework (GSC, GA4, ads, Blackbird, LinkedIn), tag GrowthX team when ready for review
